texto: You see flaws in every faceIf you look long enough.That’s why I’m so afraidOf the word ‘forever’. Forever is long enoughFor sunrises to become staleFor fire to become tameFor a favorite songTo become like nailsOn a chalkboard
autor: Forever is long enoughFor passion to waste awayLike grapes into raisinsUnder the beating sunOf countless days.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Forever is long enoughFor passion to waste awayLike grapes into raisinsUnder the beating sunOf countless days.: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “You see flaws in every faceIf you look long enough.That’s why I’m so afraidOf the word ‘forever’.…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, justin-wetch, bending-the-universe pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e bending-the-universe.
